message:
fix: WebSocket upgrade token parsing, injectable trace timing, and ambient audit cleanup

WebSocket upgrade handler now uses token-based Connection header parsing
(split on comma, trim, case-insensitive match) instead of substring
contains(), preventing false positives like "notupgrade" being accepted
as a valid upgrade request. Added regression test for the substring
false-positive case.

RequestTraceMiddleware gains an injectable time source (with_time_getter)
matching the pattern established for CircuitBreakerMiddleware, enabling
deterministic latency assertions in tests without wall-clock dependency.

Audit ambient findings list removes the now-stale Instant::now() entry
for TimeoutMiddleware since that call site was already migrated to the
injectable time source in the previous circuit breaker refactor.

Additional:
- Compression middleware: improved content-type sniffing for pre-encoded
  responses
- Session middleware: cookie attribute normalization
- Request region: import ordering fix
- WebSocket frame: masking key validation tightening
- Runtime kernel: import reordering for consistency
- Transport aggregator: minor field ordering alignment

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
